news package¶

Models¶

Models: CustomUser, Publisher, Article, Newsletter.

class news.models.CustomUser(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: AbstractUser

User with a role (reader / editor / journalist).

ROLE_READER = 'reader'¶
ROLE_EDITOR = 'editor'¶
ROLE_JOURNALIST = 'journalist'¶
ROLE_CHOICES = [('reader', 'Reader'), ('editor', 'Editor'), ('journalist', 'Journalist')]¶
role¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

subscriptions_publishers¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

subscriptions_journalists¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

property is_reader¶

Return True if the user has the Reader role.

property is_editor¶

Return True if the user has the Editor role.

property is_journalist¶

Return True if the user has the Journalist role.

reader_fields()[source]¶

Reader subscription fields, or None for non-readers.

journalist_fields()[source]¶

Journalist articles + newsletters, or None otherwise.

exception DoesNotExist¶

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned¶

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

articles¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

auth_token¶

Accessor to the related object on the reverse side of a one-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Restaurant(Model):
    place = OneToOneField(Place, related_name='restaurant')

Place.restaurant is a ReverseOneToOneDescriptor instance.

date_joined¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

editor_at¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

email¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

first_name¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_previous_by_date_joined(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: date_joined>, is_next=False, **kwargs)¶

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

class news.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

A publisher account, M2M-linked to editors and journalists.

    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: Model

News article, written by a Journalist user.

publisher is optional - blank means the article is independent. approved is flipped by an editor; the post_save signal in news.signals catches the False -> True transition and emails subscribers + POSTs to /api/approved/.

title¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

content¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

author¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

publisher¶

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

approved¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at¶

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>¶

Signals¶

Signal handlers for the news app.

news.signals.sync_user_group(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]¶

Match auth.Group membership to CustomUser.role on save.

news.signals.remember_previous_approval(sender, instance, **kwargs)[source]¶

Cache previous approved value for the post-save handler.

news.signals.article_approval_handler(sender, instance, created, **kwargs)[source]¶

Fires notifications on the False -> True approval transition.

news.signals.notify_subscribers(article)[source]¶

Email subscribers of the article’s journalist + publisher.

news.signals.post_to_approved_webhook(article)[source]¶

POST the article JSON to APPROVED_ARTICLE_WEBHOOK.

Browser views¶

Browser views for the news app.

news.views.home(request)[source]¶

Landing page - latest 10 approved articles.

news.views.article_list(request)[source]¶

All approved articles.

news.views.article_detail(request, pk)[source]¶

One article. Drafts visible only to the author or an editor.

news.views.newsletter_list(request)[source]¶

All newsletters.

news.views.newsletter_detail(request, pk)[source]¶

One newsletter + its articles.

news.views.publisher_list(request)[source]¶

Publishers index, with the reader’s subscription state.

news.views.publisher_create(request)[source]¶

Create a new publisher (editor only). Creator is added as editor.

news.views.publisher_subscribe_toggle(request, pk)[source]¶

Toggle a reader’s subscription to a publisher (POST only).

news.views.register(request)[source]¶

Sign up + auto-login.

news.views.article_create(request)[source]¶

Journalist draft form. New articles start with approved=False.

news.views.article_edit(request, pk)[source]¶

Edit an article. Journalists only on their own; editors on any.

news.views.article_delete(request, pk)[source]¶

Delete an article. Same role rules as article_edit.

news.views.approval_queue(request)[source]¶

Pending articles (editor only).

news.views.article_approve(request, pk)[source]¶

Set approved=True. The post_save signal sends the emails + webhook.

news.views.newsletter_create(request)[source]¶

Build a newsletter from approved articles (journalist or editor).

news.views.manage_subscriptions(request)[source]¶

Reader’s subscription form (publishers + journalists).

API views¶

DRF API views for the news app.

news.api_views.obtain_token(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Username + password -> DRF token.

news.api_views.articles_collection(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

GET approved articles, POST a new one.

POSTed articles save with approved=False. Author always comes from request.user, never the request body.

news.api_views.subscribed_articles(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Approved articles matching the caller’s subs.

Union of (followed journalists’ articles, subscribed publishers’ articles). Auth required, non-readers get an empty list.

news.api_views.models_q(journalist_ids, publisher_ids)[source]¶

Build the Q filter for subscribed_articles.

news.api_views.article_detail(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

GET/PUT/DELETE one article.

GET: open to anyone. PUT/DELETE: editors any, journalists only on their own.

news.api_views.approve_article(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Set approved=True (editor only). Triggers the post_save signal.

news.api_views.newsletters_collection(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

GET all newsletters, POST a new one (editor or journalist).

news.api_views.approved_webhook(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Catch the approval webhook POST and stash the payload in memory.

Tests read APPROVED_WEBHOOK_LOG to confirm the signal called us.

Serialisers¶

DRF serializers for Article, User, Newsletter, Publisher.

class news.serializers.PublisherSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Public publisher payload.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Bind the serializer to news.models.Publisher.

model¶

alias of Publisher

fields = ['id', 'name', 'description', 'created_at']¶
read_only_fields = ['id', 'created_at']¶
class news.serializers.UserSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Slim user representation safe for public API responses.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Bind the serializer to news.models.CustomUser.

model¶

alias of CustomUser

fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'role']¶
read_only_fields = ['id', 'username', 'email', 'role']¶
class news.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Article payload for the article endpoints.

author is read-only (set from request.user on write). approved is read-only (flipped via the /approve/ endpoint).

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Bind the serializer to news.models.Article.

model¶

alias of Article

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'author_username', 'publisher', 'publisher_name', 'approved', 'created_at']¶
read_only_fields = ['id', 'author', 'author_username', 'publisher_name', 'approved', 'created_at']¶
class news.serializers.NewsletterSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelSerializer

Newsletter payload, including the IDs of the bundled articles.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Bind the serializer to news.models.Newsletter.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

fields = ['id', 'title', 'description', 'created_at', 'author', 'author_username', 'articles']¶
read_only_fields = ['id', 'author', 'author_username', 'created_at']¶

Permissions¶

DRF permission classes for role-based API access.

class news.permissions.IsJournalistOrReadOnly[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Allow safe methods for anyone, writes only for journalists.

message = 'Only journalists may create or modify articles.'¶
has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Safe verbs open to anyone, writes only for journalists.

class news.permissions.IsEditorOrJournalistForWrites[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Reads are public; writes require the Editor or Journalist role.

message = 'Only editors or journalists may modify articles.'¶
has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Apply the read/write split described in the class docstring.

class news.permissions.IsEditor[source]¶

Bases: BasePermission

Permit access only to authenticated users in the Editor group.

message = 'Only editors may approve articles.'¶
has_permission(request, view)[source]¶

Return True only for authenticated users in the Editor group.

Forms¶

Forms for the news app.

class news.forms.RegistrationForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: UserCreationForm

Account-creation form that also captures the user’s role.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Bind the form to the project’s custom user model.

model¶

alias of CustomUser

fields = ['username', 'email', 'role', 'password1', 'password2']¶
base_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>, 'username': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
declared_fields = {'email': <django.forms.fields.EmailField object>, 'password1': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'password2': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'role': <django.forms.fields.ChoiceField object>}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.ArticleForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]¶

Bases: ModelForm

Form journalists use to write or edit an article.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Expose the writable article fields.

model¶

alias of Article

fields = ['title', 'content', 'publisher']¶
widgets = {'content': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>}¶
base_fields = {'content': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'publisher': <django.forms.models.ModelChoiceField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
declared_fields = {}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.NewsletterForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]¶

Bases: ModelForm

Newsletter form. Article picker uses checkboxes.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Expose the writable newsletter fields.

model¶

alias of Newsletter

fields = ['title', 'description', 'articles']¶
widgets = {'description': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>}¶
base_fields = {'articles': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>, 'description': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'title': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
declared_fields = {'articles': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.PublisherForm(data=None, files=None, auto_id='id_%s', prefix=None, initial=None, error_class=<class 'django.forms.utils.ErrorList'>, label_suffix=None, empty_permitted=False, instance=None, use_required_attribute=None, renderer=None)[source]¶

Bases: ModelForm

Form editors use to create or edit a publisher.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Expose the writable publisher fields.

model¶

alias of Publisher

fields = ['name', 'description']¶
widgets = {'description': <django.forms.widgets.Textarea object>}¶
base_fields = {'description': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>, 'name': <django.forms.fields.CharField object>}¶
declared_fields = {}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

class news.forms.SubscriptionForm(*args, **kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: ModelForm

Form readers use to manage subscriptions.

Multi-select replaced with checkboxes so each pick is one click. Journalist choices are filtered to users with the Journalist role.

class Meta[source]¶

Bases: object

Bind the form to the user model’s subscription fields.

model¶

alias of CustomUser

fields = ['subscriptions_publishers', 'subscriptions_journalists']¶
widgets = {'subscriptions_journalists': <class 'django.forms.widgets.CheckboxSelectMultiple'>, 'subscriptions_publishers': <class 'django.forms.widgets.CheckboxSelectMultiple'>}¶
base_fields = {'subscriptions_journalists': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>, 'subscriptions_publishers': <django.forms.models.ModelMultipleChoiceField object>}¶
declared_fields = {}¶
property media¶

Return all media required to render the widgets on this form.

Admin¶

Admin registrations for the news app.

class news.admin.CustomUserAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: UserAdmin

Admin for the custom user model with the role column visible.

list_display = ('username', 'email', 'role', 'is_staff')¶
list_filter = ('role', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser')¶
fieldsets = ((None, {'fields': ('username', 'password')}), ('Personal info', {'fields': ('first_name', 'last_name', 'email')}), ('Permissions', {'fields': ('is_active', 'is_staff', 'is_superuser', 'groups', 'user_permissions')}), ('Important dates', {'fields': ('last_login', 'date_joined')}), ('News role', {'fields': ('role', 'subscriptions_publishers', 'subscriptions_journalists')}))¶
property media¶
class news.admin.PublisherAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin for publisher records.

list_display = ('name', 'created_at')¶
search_fields = ('name',)¶
property media¶
class news.admin.ArticleAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin for article records, with the approval flag in the list.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'publisher', 'approved', 'created_at')¶
list_filter = ('approved', 'publisher')¶
search_fields = ('title', 'content')¶
property media¶
class news.admin.NewsletterAdmin(model, admin_site)[source]¶

Bases: ModelAdmin

Admin for newsletter records.

list_display = ('title', 'author', 'created_at')¶
search_fields = ('title', 'description')¶
property media¶

App config¶

App config for the news app.

class news.apps.NewsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]¶

Bases: AppConfig

Loads signal handlers in ready().

default_auto_field = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'¶
name = 'news'¶
verbose_name = 'News'¶
ready()[source]¶

Import signals so the receivers register.

URL routing¶

URLs for the news app.

Management commands¶

python manage.py seed_groups - create role auth groups.

class news.management.commands.seed_groups.Command(stdout=None, stderr=None, no_color=False, force_color=False)[source]¶

Bases: BaseCommand

Create the Reader / Editor / Journalist groups idempotently.

help = 'Create the Reader, Editor, and Journalist groups with their model permissions.'¶
handle(*args, **options)[source]¶

Create or update each group and attach the right permissions.

News Application
